title: The Land of the Lingam
description: The Land of the Lingam is a book by Arthur Miles. It cannot be imported into India.
descriptionParagraphs: The Land of the Lingam is a book by Arthur Miles. It cannot be imported into India.

The surviving record is interesting because it shows how even ordinary-looking books can acquire a charged political afterlife. As a book, it can be read not only for subject matter but for the way form, tone, and circulation make a text feel dangerous, intimate, or politically usable to anxious officials.

It also matters as part of a wider censorship history in India. The present page is a dossier starter built from source-tracked ban records; the surviving note currently says: It cannot be imported into India. The book is about Hinduism, caste and phallicism. More publication history, translations, and close reading can be added later.

overview.englishDescription: The Land of the Lingam is a book by Arthur Miles. It cannot be imported into India.
banningParagraphs: The Land of the Lingam entered censorship debates as a book associated with controversy, publication history, and state scrutiny. In the current dossier, the main state objections cluster around political sensitivity.

The earliest event currently captured here is 1937 in India, where Government of India or British Indian authorities banned publication, sale, or possession. It cannot be imported into India. The book is about Hinduism, caste and phallicism. It cannot be imported into India. The book is about Hinduism, caste and phallicism.

counterReadings.title: 100 Banned Books: Censorship Histories of World Literature
counterReadings.author: Nicholas J. Karolides, Margaret Bald, and Dawn B. Sova
counterReadings.note: A compact reference on how censorship systems moved across states, churches, and courts.

counterReadings.title: Banned Books: 387 B.C. to 1978 A.D.
counterReadings.author: Anne Lyon Haight
counterReadings.note: Useful for comparing older obscenity, heresy, and political bans with modern free-speech disputes.
